Class FreeParticle1D

Namespace
QuantumWaves.Solutions
Assembly
QuantumLibrary.dll

Provides solutions for a free particle in one dimension.

public static class FreeParticle1D

Methods

Energy(float, float, float)

Computes the energy of a free particle given its wave number.

public static float Energy(float k, float mass, float hBar)

Parameters

k float

Wave number.

mass float

Particle mass (must be greater than zero).

hBar float

Reduced Planck constant (must be greater than zero).

Returns

float

The particle energy.

Exceptions

ArgumentOutOfRangeException

Thrown if mass or hBar is less than or equal to zero.

GaussianWavePacket(float, float, float, float, float, int, float?)

Creates an approximate Gaussian wave packet as a weighted sum of plane waves.

public static WeightedWaveFunction1D GaussianWavePacket(float x0, float sigma, float k0, float mass, float hBar = 1, int termCount = 499, float? deltaK = null)

Parameters

x0 float

Initial center position of the packet.

sigma float

Spatial width parameter of the Gaussian packet. Must be greater than zero. Smaller values produce a more localized packet in position space and a wider momentum-space distribution.

k0 float

Central wave number of the packet.

mass float

Particle mass. Must be greater than zero.

hBar float

Reduced Planck constant. Must be greater than zero.

termCount int

Number of plane wave terms used in the approximation. Must be positive and odd.

deltaK float?

Spacing between sampled wave numbers in momentum space. Must be greater than zero.

If null, a value is automatically chosen based on sigma and termCount to provide a reasonable momentum-space sampling range.

Returns

WeightedWaveFunction1D

A weighted superposition of plane waves approximating a Gaussian wave packet.

Remarks

The packet is constructed by discretizing the Fourier integral representation of a Gaussian momentum distribution.

Approximation quality depends on both the momentum-space sampling density (deltaK) and the total sampled momentum range:

kRange ≈ termCount * deltaK

Choosing a very small deltaK without increasing termCount can truncate significant portions of the Gaussian momentum distribution, producing inaccurate normalization and distorted probability densities.

For accurate results, the sampled momentum range should span several Gaussian widths in momentum space, typically:

|k - k0| ≲ 5 / sigma

Larger termCount and smaller deltaK generally improve the approximation at the cost of additional computation.

The resulting packet is approximately normalized when the sampled momentum range is sufficiently large.

Exceptions

ArgumentOutOfRangeException

Thrown if sigma, mass, hBar, termCount, or deltaK is invalid.

ArgumentException

Thrown if termCount is even.

PlaneWave(float, float, float)

Creates a plane wave solution for a free particle.

public static SeparableWaveFunction1D PlaneWave(float k, float mass, float hBar)

Parameters

k float

Wave number.

mass float

Particle mass (must be greater than zero).

hBar float

Reduced Planck constant (must be greater than zero).

Returns

SeparableWaveFunction1D

A separable wave function representing the plane wave.

Remarks

Plane waves are not square normalizable over an infinite domain.

Exceptions

ArgumentOutOfRangeException

Thrown if mass or hBar is less than or equal to zero.
